name: Salinas de la Rosa, Pozo de la Nieve – Ruta Circular – Parque Regional Sierra del Carche
region_intro: Mountain biking around Jumilla offers diverse landscapes and natural features, making it an excellent destination for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by its mountainous terrain, including ranges like Sierra del Carche, Sierra Larga, and Sierra de Santa Ana. These areas provide a blend of challenging and scenic routes, with natural parks offering extensive opportunities for exploration. The varied topography ensures a wide array of mountain biking trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Jumilla The most popular mountain bike trail is Salinas de la Rosa, Pozo de la Nieve – Ruta Circular – Parque Regional Sierra del Carche, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete, traversing the Sierra del Carche Regional Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Jumilla a Monasterio de Santa Ana del Monte – Ruta Circular, a moderate 10.4 miles (16.8 km) path. This route leads through the scenic area around the historic Monasterio de Santa Ana. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Sierra de la Pila – Monasterio de Santa Ana loop from Jumilla, a 18.8 miles (30.3 km) trail leading through the foothills of Sierra de la Pila, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Jumilla is defined by its mountainous terrain, natural parks, and diverse singletrack. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Jumilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Jumilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Jumilla?
A: Jumilla offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy routes to challenging singletracks with features like jumps and rock gardens. The region has 41 mountain bike tours, with 8 easy, 23 moderate, and 10 difficult options.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Jumilla?
A: Yes, Jumilla has several family-friendly mountain bike routes, particularly among its 8 easy-graded trails. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain, making them suitable for riders of different ages and abilities. While specific family routes aren't highlighted by name, the easier trails provide a great starting point for family outings.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while mountain biking in Jumilla?
A: Jumilla's trails often pass by significant natural and historical sites. You can explore the Sierra del Carche Regional Park, known for its beautiful flora and fauna. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936581103/jumilla-a-monasterio-de-santa-ana-del-monte-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santa Ana del Monte&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark, and the region also features ancient cave paintings and 7-million-year-old fossil footprints in areas like Sierra de La Cingla and Sierra de La Pedrera. For specific points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4095975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Carche Summit and Mountain Refuge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Jumilla?
A: Absolutely. Many of Jumilla's mountain bike trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e920528063/salinas-de-la-rosa-pozo-de-la-nieve-ruta-circular-parque-regional-sierra-del-carche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de la Rosa, Pozo de la Nieve – Ruta Circular – Parque Regional Sierra del Carche&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 30.6 km loop. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936581103/jumilla-a-monasterio-de-santa-ana-del-monte-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jumilla a Monasterio de Santa Ana del Monte – Ruta Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.8 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Jumilla?
A: Jumilla's climate makes it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable, while winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round exploration.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails near Jumilla typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Jumilla varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936581103/jumilla-a-monasterio-de-santa-ana-del-monte-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jumilla a Monasterio de Santa Ana del Monte – Ruta Circular&lt;/a&gt; (16.8 km) can take around 1 hour and 8 minutes, while a more challenging and longer trail such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27626441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final Stretch to the Summit – Salinas de la Rosa loop from Casas del Puerto&lt;/a&gt; (50.1 km) might require about 4 hours and 26 minutes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Jumilla?
A: The mountain bike trails in Jumilla are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the natural parks, and the variety of options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails around Jumilla for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find plenty of challenging mountain bike trails in Jumilla. The region offers 10 difficult-graded routes, many of which feature significant elevation changes, technical singletracks, and exciting elements like jumps and rock gardens. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e920528063/salinas-de-la-rosa-pozo-de-la-nieve-ruta-circular-parque-regional-sierra-del-carche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de la Rosa, Pozo de la Nieve – Ruta Circular – Parque Regional Sierra del Carche&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3967771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de la Rosa loop from Jumilla&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 60 km.

Q: Can I use an e-bike on the mountain bike trails in Jumilla?
A: Yes, Jumilla is supportive of e-biking, and numerous trails in the region allow their use. This means you can enjoy the diverse landscapes and varied terrain with the assistance of an e-bike, making longer or more challenging routes accessible to a wider range of riders.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Jumilla's mountain bike trails traverse a landscape rich in natural features. You'll encounter prominent mountain ranges like Sierra del Carche, Sierra Larga, and Sierra de Santa Ana. The Sierra del Carche Regional Park is particularly notable for its beautiful flora and wild fauna. Other areas, such as Sierra de La Cingla and Sierra de La Pedrera, offer stunning natural environments and geological points of interest.

Q: Are there any mountain refuges or huts along the trails for breaks?
A: Yes, some trails in Jumilla pass by mountain refuges or huts that can offer a place to rest. For example, you might find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4095975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Carche Summit and Mountain Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3965357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Pelado Shelter&lt;/a&gt;. These can be useful for longer rides or as points of interest.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Many of Jumilla's mountainous trails provide excellent opportunities for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14630210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de la Pila – Monasterio de Santa Ana loop from Jumilla&lt;/a&gt; is specifically named for its scenic vistas. Additionally, the Jumilla Castle, perched atop a hill, offers sweeping views of the town and surrounding landscapes, and some trails in the Sierra del Carche Regional Park lead to high points with expansive outlooks.
tips:
From its top at 1370 meters you will have amazing views of the valleys of the Altiplano de Murcia and beyond! There are many alternative paths to reach the top with more or less difficulty but all of them are very attractive. Remember to bring plenty of water and suitable footwear, if you prefer some walking sticks will always help.
At the top is the mountain refuge to spend the night and enjoy wonderful sunsets and sunrises!

This snow well is a construction of traditional architecture with great ethnographic importance. Its construction began in 1683. The snow/ice was stored for later transport to nearby towns, intended for use both in food preservation and in the field of medicine.
Salt exploitation in the heart of the Sierra del Carche.
